title: Columns - The Kathmandu Post
authors: By Pramod Regmi & Edwin van Teijlingen
summary: Nepal’s food system is under strain. Are we prepared to safeguard our future? By Madhukar Upadhya Nepal urgently needs a comprehensive, long-term strategy to safeguard its food security. content:
Nepal’s food system is under strain. Are we prepared to safeguard our future? By Madhukar Upadhya Nepal urgently needs a comprehensive, long-term strategy to safeguard its food security. AI: Modern-day Prometheus or monster? By Kim Seong-kon AI will constantly evolve due to its deep-learning capability. Someday, it may overcome its limits and outsmart humans. Such a phenomenon will inevitably change the whole terrain of our society. Doing away with exams, ushering in assessments By Salome Bhatta If exams fail to serve their very purpose, how should we actually assess learning? Open-air burning is choking our cities. It’s time to tackle it By Sophia L Pandé People must be educated about the harm caused by open-air burning to themselves and others. The essence of statecraft is not economics By Sucheta Pyakuryal Economic prosperity is part and parcel of statecraft, not the be-all and end-all of it. The American-Israeli war on Iran is the quest for dominance By Kashif Islam The talks in Pakistan aim not for peace but to buy time and weaken Iran permanently. Wealth, professionalism and political responsibility in Bikram Sambat 2082 By Deepak Thapa The nationwide celebration of a young Madheshi woman as Deputy Speaker of HoR offers hope. Improving ambassador appointments By Jhabindra Aryal It is vital to appoint career diplomats as ambassadors to advance our national interests. Beyond history: Unlocking new avenues in Nepal-UK cooperation By Chandra Ghimire Now is the moment to develop strategic actions aligned with technical competence and national interests. Nepal’s migrant workers: Data the new government can’t ignore By Pramod Regmi & Edwin van Teijlingen Undocumented workers, arguably among the most vulnerable, remain invisible in official statistics. Breaking down Nepal’s health budget: Where are the funds going? By Khim Lal Devkota Without reforms, Nepal’s federal health system risks remaining centralised. Enduring legacy of Ambedkar. Lessons for Nepal By Prashant Negi & Rup Sunar RSP signals reform, but Ambedkar’s framework questions Dalit inclusion within Nepal’s evolving governance. How the government silently abandoned the proposed apology to Dalits By Mitra Pariyar The dismissal of the state’s apology to Dalits discredits both the government and the RSP. Tourism, culture and development: Three pillars of Nepal’s soft power By Prasanta Kumar BK Now is the time to unlock Nepal’s soft power globally and ensure its progress isn’t delayed. In times of market uncertainty, sustained economic growth is the right medicine stocks need By Paban Raj Pandey Policies focused on sustained growth will eventually reflect in stock prices. The ventilator economy gets its chance—it’s time to act By Himesh Dhungel Exhausted population awaits quick, tangible and meaningful results on the government’s growth and governance promise. How Mundhum is shaping political consciousness among the Kirat people By Abhi Subedi The Mundhum provides a basis for forming political perspectives among the Kirat people. Petrol to EV conversion sounds easy. Is it actually safe? By Bishal Silwal We cannot afford to treat the electrification of the old fleet as a trial-and-error experiment. Nepali women are rewriting our story. Are we ready to listen? By Patricia Fernandez-Pacheco When women tell their own stories, they do more than describe the world; they reshape it. Foreign interference exists in Nepal. And it can be managed By Naresh Koirala Unfortunately, we lack the legal mechanisms and resources to combat interference.

By Madhukar Upadhya

Nepal urgently needs a comprehensive, long-term strategy to safeguard its food security.

By Kim Seong-kon

AI will constantly evolve due to its deep-learning capability. Someday, it may overcome its limits and outsmart humans. Such a phenomenon will inevitably change the whole terrain of our society.

By Khim Lal Devkota

By Prashant Negi & Rup Sunar
